---
title: "Pillars of the Earth"
subtitle: "A Narrator's Companion"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

A six-evening storytelling guide to Ken Follett's *The Pillars of the Earth* — one of the great historical novels of the 20th century. Built for the kind of friend who'll listen over dinner across six nights, not the kind who'll read 1,000 pages.

This site contains:

- A **historical-context primer** — the real-world hierarchies, events, and institutions the novel sits on top of (the Anarchy, Becket's murder, Gothic architecture, feudal ranks, the wool trade).
- A series of **20-minute spoken narrations**, one per Part of the book, with delivery cues and natural cliffhangers.

This is a *companion*, not a substitute. It does not reproduce the novel.

---

## How to use this

1. **Read [the Context page](Context.md) once** before you start. It's the world the story lives in.
2. **Rehearse Day 1 out loud once** before delivering it. Twenty minutes goes faster than you think.
3. **Deliver one Day per evening.** The book's own Parts end on cliffhangers — don't fight that structure.
4. **Pause where the cues say to pause.** Storytelling forgives a long pause; it does not forgive a panicked rush.

---

## The six evenings

| Day | Part | Spine | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **[1](Day%201%20-%20The%20Hanging%20and%20the%20Snow.md)** | I — Tom Builder | Tom loses everything, Agnes dies, Ellen takes the baby, the cathedral burns | Ready |
| **[2](Day%202%20-%20The%20Fall%20of%20the%20House%20of%20Shiring.md)** | II — Fall of the House of Shiring | Aliena's father falls, William sacks the castle, her vow, ground is broken | Ready |
| **[3](Day%203%20-%20Building.md)** | III — Building | Jack's genius, Aliena's wool empire, the wool fair attack, Tom's death | Ready |
| **[4](Day%204%20-%20Love%20and%20Vengeance.md)** | IV — Love and Vengeance | Jack and Aliena, the forced marriage, Jack walks toward Spain | Ready |
| **[5](Day%205%20-%20The%20Mystery.md)** | V — The Mystery | Gothic architecture, the truth about Jack's father, reunion | Ready |
| **[6](Day%206%20-%20Reckoning.md)** | VI — Reckoning | Becket's murder, William hanged, Waleran broken, cathedral consecrated | Ready |

---

## The opening hook

Every good telling of this book starts the same way: with a hanging, a curse, and a question that takes 800 pages to answer.

Go to **[Day 1 →](Day%201%20-%20The%20Hanging%20and%20the%20Snow.md)** when you're ready.
